title: Venezuela rallies past Italy to make first WBC final

title: KD says loss to Lakers on him: 'I'm the offense'

content: The Jets have agreed to trade quarterback Justin Fields to the Chiefs, pending a physical, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter on Monday.

content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

content: The officials later lodged a complaint with the Tiruchengode Rural police against TVK youth wing secretary Madeswaran. The police registered a case against him.

title: I was thrown into jail, with no communication with my legal team or family, says Sonam Wangchuk
content: In his first interaction after his release, Mr. Wangchuk said, both the government and people have to be flexible on demands; “Talks are a give and take process, both sides have to be flexible and considerate, “ he said and warned, unnecessary prolonging of the dialogue, would hamper meaningful conversation

title: Panel submits report on Uniform Civil Code to Gujarat CM Bhupendra Patel
content: Gujarat government is likely to table a draft Bill on UCC in the legislature Assembly during the ongoing Budget session

title: Edappadi Palaniswami criticises DMK government for not filing FIR on cash-for-jobs complaint
content: As no follow up had been done, the AIADMK had approached the High Court with a prayer for filing the FIR, stated the AIADMK leader

title: ‘Not for all LPG consumers’: Govt clarifies on eKYC norms
content: The clarification came as some news reports have been circulating on the issue of biometric aadhaar authentication for LPG consumers

title: Refrain from self-promotion, impulsive posts: Centre’s premier training academy to new civil services officers
content: The advisory for the new civil services candidates has been issued by Mussoorie-based Lal Bahadur Shastri National Academy of Administration (LBSNAA); the civil services exam result was announced on March 6

title: PMK’s Anbumani vs. Ramadoss: Madras High Court directs civil court to hear dispute after May 10
content: Justice T.V. Thamilselivi passes the order after taking note that elections have been notified for Tamil Nadu and Puducherry and hence hearing an intra party dispute at this stage may not be appropriate

title: Congress reiterates demand for all‑party meeting on Women’s Reservation Act rollout
content: Government reaches out again, this time in writing, after Congress spurns its informal feelers to discuss the implementation timeline for the Women’s Reservation Act

title: Postal department launches premium services with 24-hour, 48-hour delivery guarantee
content: Launching the services — 24 Speed Post, 24 Speed Post Parcel, and 48 Speed Post — Communications Minister Jyotiraditya Scindia said India Post's revenue growth is encouraging

title: It’s Mamata vs. Suvendu again; Trinamool to contest 291 seats in Bengal polls
content: Ms. Banerjee said her party will contest 291 of the 294 seats. “The remaining three seats will be contested by our ally, Anit Thapa-led BGPM, in the Darjeeling hills,” she said

title: Parliamentary panel suggests a raise to EPF pension, says ₹1,000 inadequate to meet basic needs
content: This assumes significance in view of pensioners' demand to raise the pension to ₹7,500 per month, as ₹1,000 is not sufficient to make both ends meet

title: Electric RTC buses will reduce daily CO2 emissions of 1,200 tons: TGSRTC MD Nagi Reddy
content: Outlining the roadmap, TGSRTC V-C and MD V. Nagi Reddy said that between 2025 and 2030, about 35% of the TGSRTC fleet is expected to transition to electric

title: EC should start campaigning for BJP: Mamata
content: Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee said the upcoming election was a “fight for survival” for Bengal; she said the party will contest 291 seats in the West Bengal polls and will win more than 226 seats

title: ECI transfers 19 senior police officers in poll-bound West Bengal
content: After the poll dates were announced on Sunday (March 15), the ECI transferred Chief Secretary Nandini Chakravorty and Home Secretary Jagdish Prasad Meena

title: T.N. Assembly poll: Election Commission of India deputes general, police, expenditure observers for State
content: These 136 general observers, 40 police observers and 151 expenditure observers would be stationed in their respective Assembly constituencies by March 18, 2026

title: No government job through unemployment offices in five years in Rajasthan; 22 lakh candidates registered: RTI
content: The data, provided by the Directorate of Employment, shows that as of January 14, a total of 22,21,317 candidates were registered as job seekers in district employment offices across the State

title: Actor Kamal Haasan extends wishes to Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan ahead of elections
content: ”Let us make the right decision. Comrade Vijayan vijayikkatte. Keralamum Vijayikkum” wrote Mr. Haasan. Mr. Vijayan in his reply posted in X thanked the actor for his wishes and constant support

title: Bengaluru NGO helps replenish water sources inside Turahalli forest
content: Operation Quench Thirst initiative by PfA Wildlife Hospital focuses on replenishing critical water sources inside Turahalli Forest through daily water tanker deployments and installation of water troughs while also refilling and sustaining existing natural water sources.

title: Parliamentary panel asks government to expedite monetisation tailings dumps at Bharat Gold Mines
content: The Standing Committee on Coal, Mines and Steel said in its latest report that approximately 33 million tonnes of tailings dumps at KGF, containing recoverable quantities of gold and other precious metals, have been identified for monetisation

title: Bioengineering technique can protect coast from erosion, says professor in IIT Indore
content: Experimental findings showed that bio-mediated treatment significantly improved the strength and durability of coastal sand, professor in IIT Indore explained

title: Speaker Om Birla asks Centre to form groups and brief MPs on crop insurance scheme
content: The crop insurance programme provides farmers with affordable insurance coverage against losses caused by natural calamities and other risks affecting crops

title: Lokayukta orders payment of ₹50.24 lakh EPF and ESI dues to KGBV outsourcing staff
content: ‘Authorities concerned initiated action, including filing of criminal cases against the agencies and recovery of the proceedings,’ says Registrar, Institution of Lokayukta

title: RRS chief urges Karnataka not to increase Almatti dam height
content: RRS president Dr. Kuncham Venkatasubba Reddy said he had written a letter to PM Narendra Modi and also apprised Karnataka State water resources department Minister D.K. Shiva Kumar about the problems that the farmers of the three States face if the height of Almatti dam is increased

title: Bengaluru airport introduces Connections by BLR for seamless transfer experience
content: Transfers currently account for around 15% of total passenger traffic at Kempegowda International Airport and are expected to rise to 20% by 2026, as seamless transfers become increasingly important for both passengers and airline network planning.

title: Adoptive mothers entitled to maternity leave regardless of child’s age, says Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court also asked the Centre to come out with a provision recognising paternity leave as a social security benefit

title: Telangana prioritises public transport and EV mobility at summit
content: Ponnan Prabhakar said that the government is prioritising the strengthening of bus services, metro, and last-mile connectivity

title: Karnataka II PU exam: Students get wrong question paper in Hassan’s Belur
content: In place of the question paper meant for fresh candidates, the officials at the examination centre gave them the question paper, prepared for the repeaters.

title: Congress condemns Pakistani strike on Afghanistan hospital, says world must reject such 'barbarism'
content: "On behalf of the Indian National Congress, we are deeply distressed by the horrific loss of civilian lives in Kabul following the recent air strikes by Pakistan on a hospital, which killed nearly 400 people," says Mallikarjun Kharge

title: India’s second LPG carrier ‘Nanda Devi’ arrives at Gujarat port
content: ‘Nanda Devi’ has docked at Vadinar port in Devbhumi Dwarka district, and preparations were being made to shift the LPG to the daughter ship, Deendayal Port Authority Chairman Sushil Kumar Singh told reporters

title: Food supply jitters could trigger WFH, impact hostel occupancy, warns IT corridor hostel association
content: The association said it had received information that some IT firms are weighing remote work owing to uncertainties around food supply

title: Railways to run express trains to clear additional rush during Ugadi festival
content: The trains will halt at Tumakuru, Arsikere, Kadur, Chikjajur, Chitradurga, Rayadurg, Ballari Cantonment, and Tornagallu stations in both directions.

title: We will support anti-BJP government: AIUDF MLA Rafiqul Islam ahead of Assam polls
content: “If there is an anti-BJP government here, we will support it. If we can become a part of it, then we will address the people's problems in the education sector, the health sector, and road communication," says AIUDF MLA

title: ECI orders transfer of officials in Kerala ahead of Assembly polls
content: The ECI also said that officers who have been transferred out should not be posted in any election-related assignments till the completion of the poll process

title: Cooking in LPG crisis: Hyderabad’s kitchens adapt, but at a ‘cost’
content: The switch to firewood has drawn not only environmental and health concerns but also fears of fire outbreaks in dense urban spaces

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Madras High Court defers orders in cases challenging tender conditions for live webcasting
content: Madras High Court Chief Justice’s Bench says it will pass orders on March 17 on the writ petitions filed by two surveillance companies, one based in Chennai and another in Delhi

title: Tamil Nadu adopts single-use haemodialysis policy to improve patient safety
content: The adoption of single-use dialysis policy, according to senior doctors, marks a milestone in the evolution of dialysis care in the public sector in the State

title: Uttar Pradesh ATS arrests dental student for links with ISIS online module
content: Haarish Ali, a resident of Saharanpur district, was arrested in Moradabad on March 15 during an ongoing probe into activities of ISIS-linked

title: Odisha hospital fire toll rises to 11 as one more person succumbs to burn injuries
content: The State government has constituted a judicial panel to probe the incident, and also formed a fact-finding team headed by Development Commissioner D.K. Singh

title: Lok Sabha revokes suspension of 8 opposition MPs
content: On February 3, seven Congress MPs and one CPI(M) MP were suspended for unruly behaviour following a resolution passed by the Lok Sabha during the first part of the Budget session

title: Rajinikanth debunks TVK leader Aadhav Arjuna’s claim on the actor refraining from politics
content: In December 2020, Mr. Rajinikanth announced his political entry. However, later that month, in a three-page statement, he cited the COVID-19 pandemic and his health condition to make an exit without entering politics.

title: India condemns Pakistan airstrikes on Kabul hospital
content: India has urged the international community to hold the perpetrators of the act accountable and ensure that “the wanton targeting by Pakistan of civilians in Afghanistan ceases without delay”

title: Delhi HC directs removal of content linking Union Minister Hardeep Puri’s daughter to Jeffrey Epstein
content: The court directed that videos and posts uploaded from Indian IP addresses must be taken down, while content uploaded from outside India must be blocked to prevent access within the country

title: ‘Reed’ it right: bulrush beds make it cushy for whole avian families
content: The Clamorous reed warbler is as loud as they come, but in the urban environment, it is outshouted. Weed clearing in urban habitats brings down its home, the bulrushes. Bulrushes in wetlands are not encroachments, but ‘legal homes’ to birds in the crake and rail family and warblers, so government line agencies ought to tread on them thoughtfully

title: Rajya Sabha polls: Odisha Congress suspends three MLAs for cross-voting
content: Datteswar Ray Hota, who was fielded as a common candidate by the Congress, the BJD and the Left, lost to BJP-backed hotelier and Independent, Dilip Ray

title: Supreme Court nine-judge Constitution Bench commences hearing on definition of 'industry'
content: Attorney General R. Venkataramani is advancing his submissions in the matter. On February 16, the court formulated the broad issues to be adjudicated by the nine-judge Bench

title: Watch: Two-phase elections in West Bengal comes as a relief | The Hindu Editorial
content: The people of at least one State will heave a sigh of relief now that the schedule for five Assembly elections — all in April — has been announced.

title: Charred skeletal remains of four persons found in car near Tirunelveli’s Thisaiyanvilai
content: Police said passers-by noticed the charred car amidst bushes on a mud road branching out from the Thisaiyanvilai – Aaththankarai Pallivaasal Road at Pettaikulam on March 17, 2026

content: In a separate operation, security forces recovered a cache of explosives and small arms from the Boljang Hill Range of the district

title: Watch: Raghav Chadha bats for married couples to file income tax returns jointly
content: AAP leader Raghav Chadha in the Rajya Sabha proposed optional joint filing of Income Tax returns for married couples so that households with uneven incomes are not penalised.

title: How food ushers in new beginnings at Ugadi
content: Author Ratna Rajaiah explores the symbolic and nutritional reasons behind consuming neem and jaggery during this festival

title: Parliament Budget Session LIVE: Rajya Sabha discusses the Appropriation Bill, 2026, for consideration and return
content: Rajya Sabha will bid farewell to retiring members on March 18, Question Hour and Zero Hour will not be held; Lok Sabha discusses the demands for grants under the control of the Ministry of Railways for 2026-27

title: The silent commentators at the T20 World Cup
content: Sign language interpreters from India Signing Hands made sure that deaf audience of the matches involving India “heard” them

title: ‘KD: The Devil’ song: KVN Productions takes down track after severe backlash over “vulgar” lyrics
content: Kannada director Prem and production house KVN Productions are facing criticism over the special number from their upcoming film, ‘KD: The Devil’, starring Dhruva Sarja. The song features Nora Fatehi and Sanjay Dutt. Fans are unleashing their frustration on social media, slamming the lyrics as “vulgar” and “suggestive”

title: Light rain forecast for Mumbai, neighbouring districts on March 18-19
content: For the rest of Maharashtra, the IMD has predicted moderate summer conditions for the next four to five days

title: Ten injured after pharma company bus overturns in Miyapur
content: According to the police, the mini-bus belonging to Lee Pharma overturned on the road near the Tata Motors stretch towards Bachupally

title: Arjun Krish and Ria Dinesh on winning the award for the Best Telefilm at Kerala State Television Awards
content: Their short film F for Freedom, told through the eyes of a homemaker, tackles themes such as individuality, alienation, patriarchy, and sexuality

title: Freedom of Religion Bill passed in Maharashtra Assembly; 'not against any community', says Fadnavis
content: As per the Bill, those involved in unlawful conversions on the pretext of marriage will be punished with imprisonment of seven years and shall also be liable for a fine of ₹1 lakh

title: Rajya Sabha polls: BJP’s Sanjay Bhatia, Congress’ Karamvir Boudh win two remaining seats
content: The Election Commission invalidated the vote of Congress MLA Paramvir Singh on the complaint of the BJP over the violation of vote secrecy

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Poll heat rises in Palakkad as BJP trains guns on UDF MLA scandal
content: The BJP has fielded Sobha Surendran, who helped the party leap to the second position in the constituency for the first time in 2016, hoping to wrest the seat from the United Democratic Front. The Left Democratic Front has been finishing in third spot in the segment for the past three elections. Neither the UDF nor the LDF has announced candidates for the segment

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Political crossovers gather pace in Kerala as Assembly elections near
content: Defections and cross-affiliations are blurring the traditional ideological boundaries between the United Democratic Front, Left Democratic Front, and the National Democratic Alliance. Increasingly, electoral pragmatism rather than ideological loyalty appears to be shaping political choices

title: Amrutanjan possessed Mylapore Kapaleeswarar Temple land till 2018, has ₹9.74 crore rental arrears: HR&CE dept. tells Madras High Court
content: The department also says there was no direct landlord-tenant relationship between the temple and the company, yet it continued to be in occupation of the property for decades together

title: Assam polls: BJP keeps tribal council-based ally in limbo
content: The BJP is yet to clear the air about the status of the United People’s Party Liberal in the National Democratic Alliance

title: BJP romps home with three Rajya Sabha seats in Odisha, BJD content with one seat
content: As many as 11 MLAs cross-voted in favour of BJP-backed independent candidate Dilip Ray, who initially did not have numerical strength for winning the Rajya Sabha seat

title: SC declines to intervene in Calcutta High Court order for NIA probe into Beldanga violence
content: On February 11, the apex court questioned the invocation of the UAPA; on March 16,the court finds the High Court’s confirmation of the NIA probe into the case ‘quite balanced’

title: All five NDA candidates win Rajya Sabha polls in Bihar, including CM Nitish Kumar and Nitin Nabin
content: Three MLAs of Congress party and one MLA of Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) skipped the voting. RJD MLA Kumar Sarvjeet alleged that BJP used the money power to win the election.

title: ‘Hope peace restores’: Maharashtra Congress president meets Consul General of Iran
content: Mr. Sapkal further said that he expressed condolences over the demise of Iran’s Supreme Leader Ali Hosseini Khamenei

title: Congress sees BJP leader’s bid to trigger polarisation
content: BJP MP from Aligarh, Satish Gautam, had claimed that Muslims were taking the most advantage of welfare schemes

title: Transgender communities across India demand withdrawal of Bill to redefine trans people
content: The Centre’s action led to protests and public meetings within the transgender community across cities such as New Delhi, Pune, Hyderabad and Kolkata, calling for protecting the right to self-perceived gender identity

title: AAP govt. fulfilled all pre-poll promises in just 4 years: Punjab CM
content: Mann says key promises include free electricity, 65,000 govt. jobs and 5.5 lakh employment opportunities; Oppn. calls it ‘most disappointing govt.’

title: Data from redBus shows steady rise in women travellers from Karnataka
content: Solo travel by women has increased by 76% compared to 2019, highlighting a strong shift towards independent journeys

title: BJP announces first list of candidates for 144 seats for Bengal Assembly polls
content: Suvendu Adhikari will contest from two seats Nandigram in Purba Medinipur district and Bhabanipur in Kolkata; at Bhabanipur, Adhikari is likely to contest against Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: NDA declares candidates for 12 seats in Kozhikode
content: Though the UDF is yet to officially announce its candidates, Congress probables who have received the green signal from top leadership have already begun campaigning. LDF candidates too have entered campaign mode

title: Why Trump's 'beautiful armada' cannot take control of the Strait of Hormuz
content: The proposal from President Trump to assemble an international naval coalition for the defense of the Strait of Hormuz encounters formidable obstacles. Iran's advanced A2/AD capabilities—featuring an array of missiles, drones, and submarines—combined with the strait's challenging topography, complicates even the strongest navy's efforts to maintain a permanent presence.

title: Stage set for Suvendu vs Mamata 2.0: TMC list out for West Bengal polls
content: Trinamool Congress revealed its candidate list for the West Bengal Assembly elections, contesting 291 out of 294 seats. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee will run from Bhabanipur, while allies will contest three seats in Darjeeling. The party expressed confidence in retaining power with a significant majority, aiming for over 226 seats.

title: US-Iran war: How LPG cylinder has become the face of Middle East mayhem's impact on India
content: Global tensions in the Middle East are directly impacting Indian households, causing a severe LPG shortage.  Rising fuel costs and disrupted shipments have led to panic bookings, long queues, and black marketing, forcing businesses to adapt and highlighting India's heavy reliance on imports.  The situation is escalating, affecting daily life and livelihoods across the nation.

title: Sunil Gavaskar faces ‘vile stuff’ attack from Pak-born player over Abrar comment
content: A controversy ignited after Sunil Gavaskar suggested Indian-owned franchises shouldn't sign Pakistani players, citing potential indirect financial contributions to India's adversaries. Pakistan-born England cricketer Azeem Rafiq fiercely condemned Gavaskar's remarks as "vile stuff." Franchise coaches defended player selections based on cricketing merit, while the BCCI stated no jurisdiction over foreign league decisions.

title: OpenAI wants to be 'Anthropic' as CEO of applications tells employees: We cannot miss this moment
content: OpenAI is pivoting from broad ambitions to focus on coding tools and enterprise clients, acknowledging competitor Anthropic's success. CEO Fidji Simo highlighted this strategic shift, calling Anthropic's rise a "wake-up call." The company is cutting 'side quests' like standalone apps to reclaim its standing with developers and businesses, aiming to integrate features like video generation into ChatGPT.

title: Salman Agha runout: Should umpires have called dead ball? MCC settles debate
content: The Marylebone Cricket Club (MCC) has confirmed a controversial run-out in the Bangladesh vs. Pakistan ODI was correctly given out under the Laws of Cricket. The non-striker was out of his ground when the wicket was broken, and the ball remained in play, making the dismissal lawful.

title: 'Use LPG wisely': Centre says supply 'matter of concern'; advises consumers not to panic
content: India's energy crisis persists due to Middle East conflict, but the government assures consumers about LPG supplies. While urging judicious consumption and alternate fuel use, authorities are conducting raids to curb hoarding and black marketing of LPG cylinders across several states.

title: Nvidia CEO seemingly 'realises' that Google, Microsoft and Meta are set to eat company's lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance in AI hardware is facing a significant challenge as customers seek cheaper, purpose-built inference chips.  Major tech companies like Google, Microsoft, and Meta are developing their own alternatives, directly competing with Nvidia's established GPU dominance. This shift signals the end of Jensen Huang's 'one chip fits all' era.

title: US gas prices: Hormuz tensions push diesel above $5/gallon, highest since Dec 2022
content: Retail diesel prices in the U.S. have surpassed $5 a gallon for the second time, impacting global economic activity and consumer goods. This surge, driven by the US-Israeli conflict with Iran and disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, presents a challenge for the upcoming midterm elections.  Gasoline prices also reached their highest since October 2023, with little sign of easing.

title: When is Eid-ul-Fitr 2026? Key dates, historical context and significance explained
content: Eid-ul-Fitr in India is anticipated on March 20 or 21, 2026, contingent on the moon sighting. This joyous festival marks the end of Ramadan's fasting and spiritual reflection.  It's a time for gratitude, community, and renewed faith, celebrating the successful completion of a month of discipline and devotion.

title: Europe bans two Chinese technology companies for 'hacker attacks', CEOs barred from entering EU
content: The European Union has imposed sanctions on two Chinese firms, Integrity Technology Group and Anxun Information Technology, along with Iran's Emennet Pasargad, for orchestrating cyberattacks against member states.  Co-founders of the Chinese companies face travel bans. These actions underscore the EU's commitment to combating malicious cyber activities and ensuring a secure digital space.

title: SC rules adoptive mothers can’t be denied maternity leave if child is older than 3 months
content: The Supreme Court has struck down a provision in the Social Security Code 2020 that limited maternity leave for adoptive mothers. The court declared the provision irrational, stating adoptive children are no different from biological ones. Consequently, adoptive mothers are now entitled to 12 weeks of maternity leave, irrespective of the child's age at adoption.

title: 'We were shocked': Trump reacts to Iran hitting US Gulf allies — was there prior warning?
content: US President Trump expressed shock at Iran's expanded strikes across the Middle East, despite US intelligence anticipating such retaliation. The conflict, now in its third week, has seen Iranian attacks on US positions and allied infrastructure, disrupting vital shipping lanes like the Strait of Hormuz.  Concerns are mounting over the war's justification and its escalating regional impact.

title: 5 IPL legends to have not won the Orange Cap
content: As IPL 2026 nears, a surprising fact emerges: several top run-scorers have never won the Orange Cap. Legends like Rohit Sharma, Shikhar Dhawan, Suresh Raina, AB de Villiers, Ajinkya Rahane, and MS Dhoni, despite their high rankings and impactful careers, have surprisingly never finished a season as the leading run-scorer.

title: Jawahar Navodaya Class 6 result out: Direct scorecard link here
content: The Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti has released the JNVST Class 6 Result 2026 for summer-bound Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas. Students who appeared in the Phase 1 entrance exam held in December 2025 can now check their qualifying status online. The selection list marks the next stage of the admission process, with shortlisted candidates required to complete document verification.

title: Gold price today: Check the price of 18K, 22K and 24K gold in your city
content: Gold prices surged on Tuesday, with April 2026 futures on MCX rising 1% to Rs 1,56,996 per 10 grams. Investors sought safe-haven assets amid escalating Middle East tensions involving the US, Israel, and Iran, which also kept oil prices above $100 per barrel. Globally, spot gold edged up slightly.

title: Watch: After Shivalik, Nanda Devi LPG tanker reaches India
content: The LPG tanker Nanda Devi arrived in Gujarat carrying 46,500 metric tonnes of LPG, set for ship-to-ship transfer. This marks the second successful LPG carrier navigating the Strait of Hormuz amid global tensions. The shipment will help manage India's LPG supply pressures.

title: ‘Kaise kheloge?’: Wife’s honest question that sparked Suryakumar’s India career
content: Suryakumar Yadav credits his wife Devisha's "brutally honest" question in 2018 as the catalyst for his international cricket career.  Her direct inquiry about his aspirations pushed him to implement disciplined lifestyle changes, leading to consistent performances and his eventual India debut. Devisha's support extended beyond cricket, focusing on mindset and humility.

title: 'We don’t need anybody': Trump asks allies to help with Hormuz crisis amid Iran war; gets snubbed
content: US President Trump expressed disappointment as key allies like Germany and Italy refused to send warships to the Strait of Hormuz. Citing legal limits and lack of mandates, these nations declined. Trump, however, asserted US strength, suggesting the request was partly a test of allied commitment amid escalating regional conflict and Iran's closure of the vital waterway.

title: ‘Khoon garam tha’: Surya on how India’s 2026 T20 WC team differed from 2024
content: Captain Suryakumar Yadav distinguished India's T20 World Cup victories, noting the 2024 team's "experienced enthusiasm" and the 2026 squad's "fiery passion of youth." While both teams possessed skill, the 2024 side leveraged seasoned players like Rohit and Kohli, whereas the 2026 unit thrived on youthful intensity and hunger, especially winning on home soil.

title: Can Iran's Russian made 'S-300' hunt Israel's 'Blue Sparrow' amid raging air battle across Gulf
content: The Russian-made S-300, first deployed in the late 1970s, is a long-range surface-to-air missile system designed to intercept aircraft, drones and incoming cruise or ballistic missiles. Over decades, it has earned a reputation as a robust shield, used by countries from Russia and Ukraine to Iran and Nato members like Greece. Its architecture relies on powerful radars such as the 30N6E2 "Tombstone" to track targets and guide interceptor missiles with high precision.

title: 'Time doesn't speak, but it waits': Rajinikanth reacts to TVK's 'DMK threat' claim
content: TVK functionary Aadhav Arjuna had alleged that the DMK family threatened actor Rajinikanth when he tried to enter politics. ​​Arjuna said he was not criticising Rajinikanth but added that Vijay had the courage to withstand such pressure. TVK organised protests at district headquarters across Tamil Nadu on Thursday, accusing the state government of failing to maintain law and order.

title: IPL 2026: Why SRH chose Ishan Kishan over Abhishek Sharma as captain
content: Ishan Kishan’s rise as a leadership contender stems from his strong domestic captaincy record, ability to manage young players, and tactical awareness as a wicketkeeper. His recent international form, successfully leading Jharkhand to the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy title, has strengthened his case, helping him get the vote of approval from the SRH management.

title: Old footbridge collapses in Delhi’s Roop Nagar; 1 dead
content: A woman died after falling into a drain when an old iron footbridge collapsed in north Delhi’s Roop Nagar, police said.

title: Gaurav Kapur breaks down as Kritika Kamra makes her bridal entry in viral video
content: On March 11, 2026, Kritika Kamra and Gaurav Kapur celebrated their love in a charming registrar wedding. As captured in viral videos, Gaurav was visibly moved as Kritika gracefully walked down the aisle, radiating happiness. Their vows were filled with genuine emotion, with Kritika referring to Gaurav as her 'sanctuary' and Gaurav promising to be her 'steady breeze. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Pistol loaded, camera rolling: Moments before Delhi property dealer shot himself dead
content: A 28-year-old property dealer tragically died after accidentally firing his cousin's licensed pistol while inspecting it. The incident, captured on video, shows the man loading the weapon and then discharging it against his chest. Police have registered a case and are investigating the accidental shooting.

title: Cardi B hospitalized after cheating ordeal as Diggs seeks reunion
content: Stefon Diggs, the popular wide receiver in the league, is facing a tough time in his career after the New England Patriots released him recently, as fans continue to troll him for allegedly cheating on Cardi B even as she gave birth to their child. Amid all the mess, Cardi B's statements about being cheated on have resurfaced and sparked panic.

title: LPG crisis hits eateries: Restaurant staff face salary cuts, layoffs
content: Middle East tensions are disrupting LPG supplies to India's food services sector, leading to operational stress. Restaurant owners warn of layoffs and salary cuts as commercial LPG access remains inconsistent, impacting smaller businesses and street vendors most severely. The industry braces for prolonged disruption with no clear recovery timeline.

title: Iran war scare: Pragg-Vaishali-Divya set for Candidates 2026 after Humpy doubt
content: Despite concerns over Koneru Humpy potentially withdrawing from the Cyprus Candidates tournament due to regional tensions, India's rising chess stars Divya Deshmukh, R Vaishali, and R Praggnanandhaa are confirmed to participate. Their teams are actively planning travel and preparations, signaling their commitment to the prestigious event as the gateway to the World Championship.

title: Babies can lie before first birthday: Why it’s normal and what it means for brain development
content: Recent studies show that even infants as young as 10 months old can display basic forms of deceit. By the time they reach three years, almost all children engage in these playful fabrications. This instinct doesn't come from a sense of right or wrong; rather, it arises from a natural drive to fulfill their desires or sidestep consequences.

title: How Mojtaba Khamenei escaped the ‘Blue Sparrow’ strike that killed his father
content: Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, narrowly survived a deadly airstrike that targeted his family and top officials. Audio reveals Mojtaba was outside his father's compound when Israeli missiles struck, killing his wife and son. The attack also claimed the lives of Ayatollah Khamenei and other senior leaders.

title: Taliban accuses Pak of bombing drug rehab centre; Islamabad says military infra hit — 10 key points
content: Pakistan airstrikes have struck a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan. Hundreds have died and many more are injured. Afghan officials accuse Pakistan of deliberately targeting civilians. Pakistan denies this, stating strikes hit militant infrastructure. Cross-border clashes continue, raising regional security concerns. Both nations present conflicting casualty figures.

title: Nora Fatehi song removed after NHRC notice, legal complaints over obscenity
content: In a heated cultural debate, the provocative track 'Sarke Chunar Teri Sarke' from the film 'KD: The Devil' has been pulled from digital platforms due to mounting legal challenges and public protests regarding its explicit nature. The National Human Rights Commission has launched an investigation into the matter. Read on to know more in detail.

title: ‘Lawrence Bishnoi 007’: Pak handlers use ‘blend-in’ tactic to track troop movement
content: Ghaziabad Police uncovered a sophisticated cross-border espionage ring using a WhatsApp group. Suspects, including youths from different religions, installed solar-powered CCTVs at Delhi Cantonment and filmed critical infrastructure in Mumbai for Pakistani handlers. This new modus operandi, focusing on blending in, highlights a dangerous shift in intelligence operations.

title: ‘My time there was over’: Samson opens up on RR exit, eyes fresh start with CSK
content: Sanju Samson, fresh from T20 World Cup heroics, revealed his Rajasthan Royals departure was a mutual feeling of a journey's end.  He's now eager to learn from MS Dhoni at Chennai Super Kings, promising to play his best against his former team despite the unusual opening fixture. Samson aims to repay Chennai's fan support.

title: Iran war impact: Sri Lanka shifts to 4-day week to conserve fuel
content: Sri Lanka has declared Wednesdays a public holiday to conserve fuel amid escalating Middle East tensions impacting global oil supplies. This move, alongside similar measures in other Asian nations like Thailand and Myanmar, aims to mitigate potential shortages. Motorists in Sri Lanka now face fuel rationing, a stark reminder of past economic struggles.

title: After Anthropic hit, Infosys, TCS & other IT stocks tank on Nvidia’s new AI system news
content: ​​Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) and HCL Technologies dropped nearly 2%, while Tech Mahindra slipped more than 1%. The declines pushed the Nifty IT index down by over 2%, making it the worst-performing sectoral index on the NSE.

title: Opinion: In Numbers: The Curious Reason Iran Eased Down Its 'Drone Swarms' After March 9
content: Based on the data compiled, we can bucket the strike pattern broadly into three distinct phases: an initial saturation attack, a sustained drone attrition campaign, and a sudden decline in attack intensity.

title: First Batch Of Indians Back, Many Still Stuck As Azerbaijan Shuts Iran Border
content: As the war broke out on February 28 with massive bombing on Tehran, borders were closed, flights were suspended and communications were severely hit.

content: US-Israel-Iran War News LIVE Updates: The war in the Middle East broke out when the US and Israel attacked Iran on February 28.

title: Rahul Gandhi's Conduct Inappropriate In Parliament, Say 200 Ex-Officials
content: Hundreds of retired bureaucrats and former military officers have accused Leader of Opposition Rahul Gandhi of undermining the dignity and decorum of parliament and demanded an apology from the Congress leader.

title: Ground Report: NDTV Visits Cafe Where Netanyahu Shot Video After Death Rumours
content: A cafe in Israel's Jerusalem has become a subject of intense speculation on social media in connection with the whereabouts of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

title: Centre Assures Steps To Tackle LPG Crisis, Court Closes Plea By Distributors
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta informed the Nagpur bench of the Bombay High Court that negotiations were underway internationally to stabilise supply.

title: The Tide Of Dissent In CPI(M) That May Hurt Pinarayi Vijayan In Kerala Polls
content: Kerala, which was recently rechristened as Keralam, will vote in a single phase on April 9. The votes for the 140-member assembly will be counted on May 4.

title: Man Kills Wife's Mother, Brother At UP Panchayat Meet, Shot Dead By Cops
content: A man accused of stabbing his wife and murdering his mother-in-law and brother-in-law during a panchayat meeting in Bareilly was killed in an encounter with the police, officials said on Tuesday.

title: Stock Market Highlights: Markets End In Green As Wall Street, Asian Peers Edge Higher
content: Sensex Today | Nifty 50 | Stock Market Highlights: GIFT Nifty indicated another positive start for Nifty50 and the Sensex.

content: Iran Israel War News: Israel Targets Iran's Security Chief Ali Larijani: 'Not Clear If He's Dead Or Injured'

title: Woman Killed After 60-Foot Iron Bridge Collapses Into Drain In Delhi
content: A 60-foot iron bridge over a drain collapsed in north Delhi's Roop Nagar area on Tuesday morning, killing a woman, officials said.

title: Towel Dance At Bengal School Sparks Row, Rights Body Issues Notice
content: The video, which is doing rounds on social media, shows two girls dancing to the song 'Mere Khwabon Mein' from the film 'Dilwale Dulhaniya Le Jayenge' at a cultural event in the school.

title: Not Gold, Not US Dollar, This Asset Has Surged The Most During Iran War
content: Since the war has triggered a sell-off of equities (considered riskier), investors are scrambling to park their money in safe-haven assets.

title: US Minors Sue Musk's X AI, Alleging Grok Generated Their Sexual Images
content: Three Tennessee litigants, including two minors, sued Elon Musk's xAI on Monday, alleging that it knowingly designed its Grok image generator to let people create sexually explicit content by using real photos of others.

title: Israel Says It Killed Iran's Basij Force Chief In Tehran Strike
content: Israel's military said on Tuesday it had killed the head of the Basij volunteer paramilitary force in a "precise strike in Tehran".

title: Iron Overbridge Collapses In Delhi's Roop Nagar, Woman Dies
content: A 60-foot iron bridge over a drain collapsed in north Delhi's Roop Nagar area on Tuesday morning, killing a woman, officials said.

title: Ex-Suvendu Adhikari Loyalist Pabitra Kar Rejoins Trinamool Ahead Of Polls
content: A former BJP panchayat chief, who was known to be close to senior party leader Suvendu Adhikari, on Tuesday rejoined the ruling TMC amid speculations that he may be pitted against the leader of the opposition in Nandigram seat in assembly polls

title: How Infighting Cost Congress Dear In Haryana Rajya Sabha Polls
content: Tensions between senior Congress leaders Bhupinder Singh Hooda and Kumari Selja resurfaced during the polls, raising serious concerns about unity within the state unit.

content: the SEC got off to a fast and hot start, with Arkansas taking its home series against Mississippi State in Fayetteville and Texas finally losing its first game of the season . the Tigers dropped two games on the road to Vanderbilt . this week's biggest riser is crosstown rival USC, which rose up 12 spots .

title: Green galore: Revisiting NBA St. Patrick's Day uniforms of the past
content: a select group of teams each year would wear special green uniforms on St. Patrick's Day . the initiative ended when Nike became the NBA's uniform supplier ahead of the 2017-18 season . instead of one-off holiday uniforms, the NBA and WNBA agreed to a 12-year contract extension .

title: Odds: United States favored to beat Venezuela in final
content: The  World Baseball Classic  final is set, and Team USA is a sizable favorite to take home its second title Tuesday, while Venezuela will need to overcome underdog odds once again to win its first. The Americans are -285 money-line favorites over the Venezuelans, which sports +225 odds for the WBC's championship game. The U.S. had opened the tournament as the odds-on -110 favorite, while Venezuela began at +900. Team USA had attracted over 50% of the handle to win the WBC title going into the quarterfinals at BetMGM, making the Americans the sportsbook's largest liability. Venezuela, meanwhile, garnered just 2.4% of the handle, fifth-most at the book. On the tournament MVP front, Americans  Aaron Judge  (+370),  Roman Anthony  (+600) and  Bobby Witt Jr . (+850) top the odds board, along with Venezuelan  Ronald Acuna Jr.  (+750).  Maikel Garcia  (18-1) and  Luis Arraez  (20-1) are also in the running from Venezuela. For latest odds go to  DraftKings Sportsbook

content: president of iran's football federation mehdi Taj says one solution is to move the team's matches to co-host Mexico . the u.s. and Israel launched air strikes on Iran, which then retaliated with attacks on U.S. bases in other Gulf countries .
